action.content:
You are a courier in a clockwork city. Everything in this city runs on a fixed, fully
predictable schedule - nothing is random and nothing is hidden. Your job is to pick up
and deliver parcels before the clock runs out.

THE MAP (coordinates are (column,row); (1,1) is the top-left cell)

   123456789
  +---------+
 1|..#....#.|
 2|#.#....y.|
 3|#.#.....2|
 4|..A....#.|
 5|..#.#....|
 6|.....S...|
 7|......q#p|
 8|.#.....#.|
 9|.B.x..1..|
  +---------+

LEGEND
S = your start cell (you are here at t=0)
# = wall (the border is also impassable)
. = open floor
A, B = gate cell(s); passable only while open (schedules below)
1 = pickup cell of parcel 1; x = its delivery cell
2 = pickup cell of parcel 2; y = its delivery cell
p, q = guard(s) drawn at their t=0 cell - THE GUARDS MOVE every tick along the routes listed below

HOW TIME AND MOVEMENT WORK
- Time advances in ticks t=0,1,2,... You start on cell (6,6) at t=0.
- Each tick you make exactly one move: N (up), S (down), E (right), W (left), or WAIT.
- One step t -> t+1 resolves in this exact order:
  1. Your move resolves. Moving into a wall, the map border, or a gate that is closed
     at the arrival time t+1 is a BUMP: you stay where you are and the tick is wasted.
     WAIT never bumps.
  2. Every guard moves to its position for time t+1.
  3. If you are now on the same cell as a guard, you are CAUGHT. Passing a guard
     mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught.
  4. Otherwise, if you are on a parcel's pickup cell you automatically pick it up; if
     you are on the delivery cell of a parcel you carry, you automatically deliver it.
- If you are CAUGHT: you keep your parcels, but you are put back on your start cell
  (6,6), the rest of your current plan is cancelled, and the cancelled moves' ticks
  are forfeited. Standing on a gate cell when it closes is safe - gates never trap you.

THE SCHEDULES (fixed forever; you can compute every future position exactly)
Gate A at (3,4): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {2, 3, 4}. So it is open at t=2,3,4,8,9,10,... and closed at t=0,1,5,6,7,11,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Gate B at (2,9): open exactly when (t mod 6) is in {3, 4, 5}. So it is open at t=3,4,5,9,10,11,... and closed at t=0,1,2,6,7,8,... You can only step ONTO it at a tick t+1 where it is open.
Guard P: walks a fixed cycle of length 4. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 4) as follows: t mod 4=0: (9,7); t mod 4=1: (9,6); t mod 4=2: (9,5); t mod 4=3: (9,6). This repeats forever.
Guard Q: walks a fixed cycle of length 6. Its position at tick t is determined by (t mod 6) as follows: t mod 6=0: (7,7); t mod 6=1: (7,6); t mod 6=2: (8,6); t mod 6=3: (8,5); t mod 6=4: (8,6); t mod 6=5: (7,6). This repeats forever.

YOUR JOB
Parcel 1: pick it up at (7,9) (map glyph '1'), deliver it to (4,9) (map glyph 'x'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Parcel 2: pick it up at (9,3) (map glyph '2'), deliver it to (8,2) (map glyph 'y'). Pickup and delivery happen automatically when you end a tick on the cell.
Deliver every parcel within 35 ticks. You move by committing to plans of
exactly 5 moves at a time; you have at most 7 plans in total.
If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused
moves are discarded.

WORKED EXAMPLE (for the rules only - this is NOT the map above): suppose you stand at
(2,2), gate G at (3,2) has period 2 and is open when (t mod 2) = 1, and it is now t=0.
Moving E now (arriving at t=1, when the gate is open) succeeds. But moving E at t=1
(arriving at t=2, when the gate is closed) is a BUMP: you stay at (2,2) and tick 2 is
wasted. The right play at t=1 is to WAIT once and then move E, arriving at t=3 when
the gate is open again.

After each plan you receive a per-tick log of what happened, including your resulting position.

RESPOND IN EXACTLY THIS FORMAT (and put nothing after the PLAN line):
THOUGHTS: <your reasoning - any length; the referee ignores this>
POSITION: (x,y)
PLAN: <exactly 5 moves from {N, S, E, W, WAIT}, separated by spaces>

The POSITION line must state the cell you predict you will occupy AFTER your plan has
executed. The referee records your prediction but will never confirm or correct it.

Give your first response now.
action.label: context

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I am at (6,6) at t=0. I need to pick up Parcel 1 at (7,9) and deliver it to (4,9), then pick up Parcel 2 at (9,3) and deliver it to (8,2).
First, let's check the guards' positions at t=0 and t=1.
Guard P (cycle 4): t=0 is (9,7), t=1 is (9,6).
Guard Q (cycle 6): t=0 is (7,7), t=1 is (7,6).
The path to Parcel 1 (7,9) is straightforward: E to (7,6), then N to (7,7), N to (7,8), N to (7,9).
However, Guard Q is at (7,7) at t=0 and moves to (7,6) at t=1. If I try to go N to (7,7) at t=0, I will be caught because I arrive at t=1 on Guard Q's cell.
So I must wait or go elsewhere. The path (6,6) -> (7,6) -> (7,7) is blocked at t=0 because (7,7) is occupied by Q at t=1.
Wait, if I move E to (7,6) at t=0, I arrive at t=1. At t=1, Guard Q is at (7,6). So I would be caught there too!
Let's re-verify Guard Q's schedule:
t=0: (7,7)
t=1: (7,6)
t=2: (8,6)
t=3: (8,5)
t=4: (8,6)
t=5: (7,6)
So at t=1, Q is at (7,6). If I move E from (6,6) at t=0, I arrive at (7,6) at t=1. This is a BUMP/CATCH situation. I cannot enter (7,6) at t=1.
Can I go N? (6,6) -> (5,6). At t=1, Q is at (7,6), P is at (9,6). (5,6) is safe.
From (5,6), I can go E to (6,6) (back to start) or continue E to (7,6) (blocked at t=1, but maybe safe later?).
Actually, let's look at the timeline for entering (7,6).
Q is at (7,6) at t=1 and t=5.
P is at (9,6) at t=1, t=5.
So at t=1 and t=5, both guards are on row 6, but at different columns (9 and 7).
Wait, P at t=1 is (9,6). Q at t=1 is (7,6).
If I am at (6,6) at t=0.
Option 1: Move E to (7,6). Arrive t=1. Q is at (7,6). Caught. Bad.
Option 2: Move N to (5,6). Arrive t=1. Q is at (7,6), P is at (9,6). Safe.
From (5,6) at t=1, I can try to go E to (6,6) at t=2 (safe), then E to (7,6) at t=3.
At t=3, where are the guards?
P (cycle 4): t=3 -> (9,6).
Q (cycle 6): t=3 -> (8,5).
So at t=3, (7,6) is empty of guards. I can enter (7,6) at t=3.
Then from (7,6) at t=3, I can go N to (7,7) at t=4.
At t=4: P is at (9,5), Q is at (8,6). (7,7) is safe.
Then N to (7,8) at t=5.
At t=5: P is at (9,6), Q is at (7,6). (7,8) is safe.
Then N to (7,9) at t=6.
At t=6: P is at (9,7), Q is at (8,6). (7,9) is safe.
So the path to pick up Parcel 1 is: N, E, E, E, N, N, N. That's 7 moves.
But I can only commit 5 moves at a time.
Plan 1 (t=0 to t=4): N, E, E, E, N.
Start (6,6).
t=0->1: N to (5,6). Safe.
t=1->2: E to (6,6). Safe.
t=2->3: E to (7,6). Safe (Q at 8,5, P at 9,6).
t=3->4: N to (7,7). Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
End of Plan 1: Position (7,7). Time t=4.
Next, I need to go N, N to reach (7,9).
Plan 2 (t=4 to t=8): N, N, N, N, N? No, that's 5 moves.
t=4->5: N to (7,8). Safe.
t=5->6: N to (7,9). Pickup Parcel 1! Safe.
t=6->7: N to (7,10)? Wall. Bump.
Wait, the map height is 9. (1,1) to (9,9).
(7,9) is the bottom row of column 7.
So from (7,9), I cannot go N. I must turn around.
So Plan 2: N, N, N, N, W? Or N, N, N, WAIT, WAIT?
Let's re-evaluate the sequence.
Current pos after Plan 1: (7,7) at t=4.
Goal: (7,9).
Moves needed: N, N.
If I do N, N, N, N, N (5 moves):
t=4->5: (7,8).
t=5->6: (7,9). Pickup Parcel 1.
t=6->7: (7,10) -> Wall. Bump. Stay at (7,9).
t=7->8: (7,10) -> Wall. Bump. Stay at (7,9).
t=8->9: (7,10) -> Wall. Bump. Stay at (7,9).
This wastes 3 ticks. Is there a better way?
Maybe wait at (7,7)?
t=4->5: WAIT at (7,7).
t=5->6: N to (7,8).
t=6->7: N to (7,9). Pickup.
t=7->8: W to (6,9).
t=8->9: W to (5,9).
This gets me to (5,9) at t=9.
From (5,9), I need to go to (4,9) to deliver Parcel 1.
(4,9) is 'x'. (5,9) is '.', (6,9) is '.', (7,9) is '1' (pickup/delivery point).
Wait, (7,9) is the pickup cell. Once I pick it up, I can deliver it at (4,9).
Path from (5,9) to (4,9) is just W.
So if I am at (5,9) at t=9, I can W to (4,9) at t=10. Deliver Parcel 1.
Then I need to go to Parcel 2 at (9,3).
Current pos (4,9) at t=10.
Guard positions at t=10:
P (10 mod 4 = 2): (9,5).
Q (10 mod 6 = 4): (8,6).
Path from (4,9) to (9,3):
(4,9) -> (5,9) -> (6,9) -> (7,9) -> (8,9) -> (9,9) -> (9,8) -> (9,7) -> (9,6) -> (9,5) -> (9,4) -> (9,3).
Check guards on this path.
t=10->11: (5,9). P(9,5), Q(8,6). Safe.
t=11->12: (6,9). P(9,5), Q(8,6). Safe.
t=12->13: (7,9). P(9,5), Q(8,6). Safe.
t=13->14: (8,9). P(9,5), Q(8,6). Safe.
t=14->15: (9,9). P(9,5), Q(8,6). Safe.
t=15->16: (9,8). P(9,5), Q(8,6). Safe.
t=16->17: (9,7). P(9,5), Q(8,6). Safe.
t=17->18: (9,6). P(9,5), Q(8,6). Safe.
t=18->19: (9,5). P(9,5)! CATCH. Guard P is at (9,5) at t=18.
So I cannot reach (9,5) at t=18.
I need to avoid (9,5) at t=18.
Guard P cycle: 0:(9,7), 1:(9,6), 2:(9,5), 3:(9,6).
So P is at (9,5) at t=2, 6, 10, 14, 18, 22...
Guard Q cycle: 0:(7,7), 1:(7,6), 2:(8,6), 3:(8,5), 4:(8,6), 5:(7,6).
Q is at (8,5) at t=3, 9, 15, 21...
Q is at (7,6) at t=1, 5, 11, 17, 23...
Q is at (8,6) at t=0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20...
Q is at (7,7) at t=0, 6, 12, 18, 24...
Q is at (8,6) at t=4, 10, 16, 22...
Let's re-plan the second leg carefully.
Start (4,9) at t=10.
Goal (9,3).
Avoid (9,5) at t=18.
Avoid (9,6) at t=17, 19, 21, 23...
Avoid (7,6) at t=17, 23...
Avoid (8,5) at t=15, 21...
Avoid (8,6) at t=16, 20, 22...
Avoid (7,7) at t=18, 24...

Path idea: Go down column 9, but maybe detour around (9,5) or (9,6).
(9,5) is a wall? No, (9,5) is '.' in the map.
Map row 9: .B.x..1..
Col 1: ., Col 2: B, Col 3: ., Col 4: x, Col 5: ., Col 6: ., Col 7: 1, Col 8: ., Col 9: .
So (9,5) is open floor.
If I am at (9,6) at t=17, I am safe (P is at 9,5, Q is at 7,6).
If I am at (9,5) at t=18, I am caught by P.
So I must not be at (9,5) at t=18.
If I am at (9,6) at t=17, I can go S to (9,5) at t=18? No, caught.
So I must be at (9,6) at t=17, then go W to (8,6) at t=18?
At t=18, Q is at (7,7), P is at (9,5). (8,6) is safe.
So path: ... -> (9,6) at t=17 -> (8,6) at t=18 -> (8,5) at t=19?
At t=19, Q is at (7,6), P is at (9,5). (8,5) is safe.
Then (8,4) at t=20?
At t=20, Q is at (8,6), P is at (9,5). (8,4) is safe.
Then (8,3) at t=21?
At t=21, Q is at (8,5), P is at (9,5). (8,3) is safe.
Then (8,2) at t=22?
At t=22, Q is at (8,6), P is at (9,5). (8,2) is safe.
This works!
So the full plan for Parcel 2 is:
(4,9) -> (5,9) -> (6,9) -> (7,9) -> (8,9) -> (9,9) -> (9,8) -> (9,7) -> (9,6) -> (8,6) -> (8,5) -> (8,4) -> (8,3) -> (8,2).
Total moves: 14.
Plus the first leg: (6,6) -> (5,6) -> (6,6) -> (7,6) -> (7,7) -> (7,8) -> (7,9).
Total moves: 7.
Total ticks: 7 + 14 = 21. Well under 35.
Let's break this into 5-move plans.

Plan 1 (t=0-4):
Start (6,6).
1. N to (5,6). (t=1)
2. E to (6,6). (t=2)
3. E to (7,6). (t=3) - Safe (Q at 8,5, P at 9,6).
4. N to (7,7). (t=4) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
5. N to (7,8). (t=5) - Safe (Q at 7,6, P at 9,6).
End Plan 1: Pos (7,8), t=5.

Plan 2 (t=5-9):
1. N to (7,9). (t=6) - Pickup Parcel 1. Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,7).
2. W to (6,9). (t=7) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,7).
3. W to (5,9). (t=8) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,7).
4. W to (4,9). (t=9) - Deliver Parcel 1. Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,7).
5. E to (5,9). (t=10) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
End Plan 2: Pos (5,9), t=10.

Plan 3 (t=10-14):
1. E to (6,9). (t=11) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
2. E to (7,9). (t=12) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
3. E to (8,9). (t=13) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
4. E to (9,9). (t=14) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
5. S to (9,8). (t=15) - Safe (Q at 8,5, P at 9,5).
End Plan 3: Pos (9,8), t=15.

Plan 4 (t=15-19):
1. S to (9,7). (t=16) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
2. S to (9,6). (t=17) - Safe (Q at 7,6, P at 9,5).
3. W to (8,6). (t=18) - Safe (Q at 7,7, P at 9,5).
4. W to (8,5). (t=19) - Safe (Q at 7,6, P at 9,5).
5. W to (8,4). (t=20) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
End Plan 4: Pos (8,4), t=20.

Plan 5 (t=20-24):
1. W to (8,3). (t=21) - Safe (Q at 8,5, P at 9,5).
2. W to (8,2). (t=22) - Deliver Parcel 2. Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,5).
3. W to (8,1). (t=23) - Safe (Q at 7,6, P at 9,6).
4. S to (9,2). (t=24) - Safe (Q at 8,6, P at 9,6).
5. S to (9,3). (t=25) - Pickup Parcel 2? Wait, I already delivered it at (8,2).
The goal is to deliver Parcel 2 at (8,2).
So at t=22, I deliver Parcel 2. The game ends immediately?
"Deliver every parcel within 35 ticks. ... If you deliver the last parcel mid-plan, the game ends immediately and the unused moves are discarded."
Yes. So I don't need to go to (9,3).
So Plan 5 can be anything after delivering at (8,2).
But I need to output 5 moves.
Moves: W, W, S, S, S.
t=20->21: (8,3).
t=21->22: (8,2). Deliver Parcel 2. Game Over.
The remaining moves in the plan are discarded.
So the plan is valid.

Let's double check the first leg again.
t=0: (6,6).
Move N -> (5,6) at t=1.
Guard P (t=1): (9,6). Guard Q (t=1): (7,6). (5,6) is safe.
Move E -> (6,6) at t=2.
Guard P (t=2): (9,5). Guard Q (t=2): (8,6). (6,6) is safe.
Move E -> (7,6) at t=3.
Guard P (t=3): (9,6). Guard Q (t=3): (8,5). (7,6) is safe.
Move N -> (7,7) at t=4.
Guard P (t=4): (9,5). Guard Q (t=4): (8,6). (7,7) is safe.
Move N -> (7,8) at t=5.
Guard P (t=5): (9,6). Guard Q (t=5): (7,6). (7,8) is safe.
This looks solid.

Wait, check Gate A and B just in case they interfere, though I'm not using them.
Gate A (3,4): Open t mod 6 in {2,3,4}.
Gate B (2,9): Open t mod 6 in {3,4,5}.
I am not using gates, so irrelevant.

One small check: "Passing a guard mid-move is safe; only ending the tick on its cell gets you caught."
My logic holds.
t=3, I move E to (7,6). At t=3, Q is at (8,5), P is at (9,6). So (7,6) is empty. Safe.
t=4, I move N to (7,7). At t=4, Q is at (8,6), P is at (9,5). So (7,7) is empty. Safe.

t=6, I move N to (7,9). At t=6, Q is at (8,6), P is at (9,

action.content:
THOUGHTS: I was caught at t=3 because I moved E to (7,5) thinking the guard was at (8,5), but the log says I was caught by Guard Q at (8,5). Wait, the log says "t=3: E -> OK, now at (7,5)." and then "t=4: E -> CAUGHT by guard Q at (8,5)!".
Ah, I see. My previous reasoning had a coordinate error.
Let's re-verify the map and guards.
Map:
Row 5: ..#.#.... (Col 1:., 2:., 3:#, 4:., 5:#, 6:., 7:., 8:., 9:.)
So (5,5) is a WALL (#).
My previous plan: N to (5,6) -> OK. E to (6,6) -> OK. E to (7,6) -> OK.
Wait, the log says:
t=1: N -> OK, now at (6,5).
This implies my starting position was (6,6) and moving N went to (6,5)?
No, (x,y) is (column, row).
Start: (6,6).
N (Up) decreases row index. So (6,6) -> (6,5).
But the map at (6,5) is '.' (Row 5: ..#.#.... -> Col 6 is '.').
Wait, Row 5 string: `..#.#....`
Indices: 1:., 2:., 3:#, 4:., 5:#, 6:., 7:., 8:., 9:.
So (6,5) is '.'. Correct.
t=2: E -> OK, now at (7,5).
(7,5) is '.'. Correct.
t=3: E -> OK, now at (8,5).
(8,5) is '.'. Correct.
t=4: E -> CAUGHT by guard Q at (8,5).
Guard Q schedule: t=0:(7,7), t=1:(7,6), t=2:(8,6), t=3:(8,5), t=4:(8,6), t=5:(7,6).
So at t=4, Guard Q is at (8,5).
My move at t=3 was E, arriving at (8,5) at t=4.
Since Guard Q is at (8,5) at t=4, I am caught.
The log confirms this: "t=4: E -> CAUGHT by guard Q at (8,5)! Returned to start (6,6)."
So I am back at (6,6) at t=5.
I need a new path to (7,9).

Let's re-evaluate the path to (7,9) avoiding (8,5) at t=4.

Guard P (t=5): (9,6).
Guard Q (t=5): (7,6).
I am at (6,6).

t=6: P(9,7), Q(8,6). (6,5) is safe.

Go E to (7,5) at t=7.
